§ 01You Did It

Dear {{recipientName}},

Congratulations. As of {{closingDate}}, {{companyName}} is an employee-owned company.

Closed
Transaction Closed Successfully
{{closingDate}}
{{esopTrustName}}
100%
§ 02What You've Built

The transaction you just completed is significant — not just for you, but for every employee of {{companyName}}. Here is what you've put in motion:

A.Employee ownership. Every eligible employee now has a beneficial interest in the company through the ESOP. Their retirement wealth is tied to the company's success.
B.Legacy preservation. The company you built will continue as an independent, employee-owned enterprise — not sold to a competitor or private equity firm.
C.Tax advantages. As a C-Corp with an ESOP, the company benefits from significant tax deductions, including the ability to deduct principal and interest on the ESOP loan.
D.Your transition. You have achieved liquidity on your terms, with a structure that aligns your financial outcome with the company's long-term success.
§ 03What Happens Next: COOP Stewardship

Your engagement with Forhemit doesn't end at closing. Beginning on Day 121, Forhemit's post-close stewardship program begins. This is the Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) — a 12-to-24-month engagement designed to ensure the employee ownership transition takes root.

Here's what to expect:

1.Days 1–120. Immediate post-close period. Focus on operational stability, employee communication, and initial COOP development.
2.Day 121. COOP Kickoff Letter. Forhemit will formally initiate the stewardship engagement with a detailed plan covering four tracks: People, Systems, Financial, and Governance.
3.Monthly check-ins. Regular calls to track progress across all four COOP tracks and address any emerging issues.
4.Quarterly reports. Written progress reports delivered to you and the ESOP trustee.
No action needed now. Forhemit will reach out before Day 121 to schedule the COOP kickoff. In the meantime, focus on the employee announcement and operational continuity.
§ 04A Personal Note

Completing an ESOP transaction is one of the most complex and consequential decisions a business owner can make. You navigated a 120-day process involving a dozen professionals, four hard-stop gates, SBA underwriting, independent appraisal, and ERISA compliance — and you did it while running your business.

That deserves recognition. On behalf of the entire Forhemit team, congratulations.

§ 05Contact

For any questions during the transition period, please contact:
